subtasks.description: Query AWS contract database to retrieve committed contract values, commitment periods, service types, and account mappings
Query AWS spend/cost database to retrieve actual monthly spend by service, account, and time period matching contract periods
Reconcile committed vs actual spend data, calculate variance ratios, identify over/undercommitted services and accounts, classify utilization posture, and generate recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks represent the minimum decomposition: (1) fetch contracts from contract warehouse (sql_gen, mid) — multi-table join across contract terms/service types; (2) fetch spend from cost database (sql_gen, mid) — aggregation across time periods and dimensions; (3) cross_recon (frontier) because reconciliation requires comparing two distinct data sources (contract vs spend), calculating utilization percentages, detecting anomalies across service categories, classifying commitment posture (overcommitted/underutilized/optimal), and generating actionable recommendations — this is high-complexity signal fusion requiring frontier reasoning capability.

output.sql: WITH aws_contracts AS (SELECT contract_id, vendor_name, SUM(amount_usd) AS committed_amount, MIN(invoice_date) AS commit_start, MAX(due_date) AS commit_end FROM invoice_facts WHERE vendor_name ILIKE '%AWS%' OR vendor_name ILIKE '%Amazon Web Services%' GROUP BY contract_id, vendor_name), aws_spend AS (SELECT publisher, product, SUM(amount_usd) AS actual_spend FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher ILIKE '%AWS%' OR publisher ILIKE '%Amazon%' GROUP BY publisher, product) SELECT c.contract_id, c.vendor_name, c.committed_amount, c.commit_start, c.commit_end, s.product, s.actual_spend, (c.committed_amount - COALESCE(s.actual_spend, 0)) AS variance, CASE WHEN s.actual_spend > c.committed_amount THEN 'Overcommitment' WHEN s.actual_spend < c.committed_amount * 0.8 THEN 'Underutilization' ELSE 'On Track' END AS status FROM aws_contracts c LEFT JOIN aws_spend s ON c.vendor_name ILIKE '%' || s.publisher || '%' ORDER BY variance DESC

SELECT publisher, product, DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) AS month, SUM(amount_usd) AS actual_spend_usd, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_seats FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'AWS' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY publisher, product, DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) ORDER BY month, product
